factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (Iran-US)
factors.summary: US strikes Iranian military site for second time in three days; Kuwait under drone/missile attack; Strait of Hormuz disruption fears elevate. Fresh escalation contradicts ceasefire narrative and threatens energy stability.

factors.name: Oil price spike on Hormuz risk
factors.summary: WTI jumped to $89.35 on fresh strikes; energy inflation persists despite ceasefire hopes. Fed officials (Goolsbee, Kashkari, Lane) flagging energy shock as persistent inflation driver; second-round effects could lock in higher prices.

factors.name: Fed inflation hawkishness
factors.summary: Kashkari, Cook, and Jefferson all emphasize inflation remains 'too high' and labor market is resilient. Cook prepared to raise rates if inflation doesn't ease; Jefferson says 'monetary policy well-positioned' but hasn't prejudged June. Rate hike risk rising despite no imminent decision yet.

factors.name: Fed communications opacity
factors.summary: Jefferson explicitly states June FOMC meeting not prejudged; no consensus on hike timing. BOJ uncertainty (Nomura says June/July hike still 'up in air' on Iran) creates global policy fog.

factors.name: Dow component earnings mixed
factors.summary: Snowflake surged 40% on AWS deal and AI acceleration; Heico beat. But Marvell, Salesforce disappointed on guidance; Zscaler crashed 31% on outlook miss. Narrow rally benefiting big AI names but broader Dow exposure heterogeneous.

factors.name: Dollar strength on risk-off
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 4-week high (~159.60), USD/CAD near highest since April 13 (~1.3800s), EUR/USD below 1.1650 on Iran escalation. Strong dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure.

factors.name: China crude demand collapse
factors.summary: Chinese oil imports set to drop to pandemic lows per Bloomberg; Shanxi coal disaster rippling through steel/power/chemicals. CAT and industrials exposed to weak China capex signals (though Australia data centre spend is offset by household spending collapse).

factors.name: Equities remain near record close
factors.summary: Wall Street posted record highs pre-strike escalation. But 4h change -0.01%, 24h change -0.29% suggests market hasn't decisively repriced Iran risk yet; move is incipient, not extended.

factors.name: Real yields / rate expectations
factors.summary: 10-year JGB yield rose to 2.72% on fiscal concerns + BOJ hike speculation. Long-end US yields (not explicitly given but implied by global moves) rising, pressuring Dow dividend and financial valuations.

factors.name: Supply chain / tariff uncertainty
factors.summary: Trump administration in talks to fund drone companies (geopolitical escalation spending), but broader tariff regime unclear. Panama imposing stricter multinational requirements adds compliance costs for Dow global players (BA, CAT, GS, etc.).

screening.narrative: The Dow faces conflicting forces: fresh Iran-US military escalation (strikes, Hormuz threats, Kuwait attacks) is reigniting energy/inflation fears and strengthening the dollar, while Fed officials uniformly signal inflation remains their top priority—leaving rate-hike risk alive despite June meeting unresolved. Narrow AI earnings beats (Snowflake, Heico) mask broader guidance disappointments (Marvell, Salesforce, Zscaler) and weakness in China demand, pressuring the Dow's industrial and international exposure. The index has barely moved intraday (-0.01% in 4h), suggesting the market is still pricing the escalation; conviction is low because Fed policy direction and Hormuz resolution both remain genuinely uncertain.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE inflation data (imminent per news, likely today or tomorrow — critical to Fed June decision)
Iran-US ceasefire negotiations update (ongoing, high daily volatility potential)
Dow component earnings (MSFT, JPM, GS, UNH, BA likely within 2-3 weeks)
ISM Manufacturing PMI / ISM Services PMI (next monthly, early June — proxy for Dow industrial health)
China economic data (PMI, factory output — CAT, DE sensitivity)
Oil price action / geopolitical escalation (Strait of Hormuz, shipping disruptions)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US ceasefire is reaffirmed or peace deal announced, energy-driven bearish case collapses and Dow rallies hard (inverse move). If PCE comes in cool (e.g., <2.8% YoY), Fed hawkishness recedes and rate-hike risk deflates, favoring equities. Conversely, if Hormuz blockade actually occurs or PCE surprises hot, downside accelerates and 50,200 support could break.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is early-stage pricing Iran escalation (move not extended yet). SELL on retest of 50,700–50,800 resistance if Hormuz tensions remain acute; BUY only on retest of 50,400 support with explicit ceasefire signal. Hold neutral pending PCE data and FOMC signaling clarity—confidence rises only once one of those two releases provides directional clarity.

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Escalation
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran overnight (May 27-28) with Iranian retaliatory response against Kuwait and threats of 'more decisive' action. Markets are pricing in renewed Strait of Hormuz disruption risk and ceasefire deal collapse, with oil jumping ~3% and risk-off sentiment across equities.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike & Energy Inflation
factors.summary: WTI rebounded from three-week lows to reclaim $91+; Fed's Goolsbee warned that oil shock combined with AI hype inflation expectations is forcing rates higher. This pressures dividend-heavy Dow names (utilities, REITs) and increases input costs for industrials.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Tone Persists
factors.summary: Kashkari (Minneapolis Fed) reaffirmed inflation fight takes priority despite decent labor market; Goolsbee cautioned on persistent energy inflation and AI-driven rate expectations. Core PCE release expected Thursday (May 28) at 12:30 GMT — likely ticking up per FXStreet note, reinforcing hawkish shift.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Rising on Oil/Geopolitics
factors.summary: 10-year JGB yields rose 3bp to 2.72% on fiscal concerns and BOJ rate hike speculation; US Treasuries retreated as oil climbed. Higher real yields headwind for equities; rate-sensitive Dow dividend payers face valuation pressure.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Safe-Haven Bid)
factors.summary: DXY jumped to near 99.50 on geopolitical tensions; USD/JPY hit four-week high ~159.60. Strong dollar pressures multinational earnings (Dow has ~50% intl revenue) through translation effects; headwind for Boeing, CAT, and other exporters.

factors.name: Asia-Pacific Market Weakness
factors.summary: ASX 200 down 1.43%, Taiwan Weighted down 0.27%, broader risk-off in Asia. While geographically separate, signals broad appetite contraction likely to carry into US session; negative spillover into Dow open expected.

factors.name: China Growth & Supply Chain Risk
factors.summary: Shanxi coal mine disaster reported to ripple through steel, power, chemicals; China facing early heavy rains with flood risk to crop belt. Supply tightness and input cost pressure for commodity-sensitive Dow names (CAT, MMM, HON materials exposure).

factors.name: PCE Inflation Data Imminent (Thursday 12:30 GMT)
factors.summary: Core PCE for April due this session; analysts expect upside tick, which would confirm Fed's recent hawkish guidance and likely trigger another equity selloff leg. Major catalyst hours away — creates choppy conditions into release.

factors.name: Intraday Price Action Flat
factors.summary: 4h change -0.01%, 24h change -0.29% — minimal move despite headline chaos. Suggests market is waiting for PCE data or deal clarity on Iran before committing directionally. Low conviction selling so far.

factors.name: Boeing China Deal (Positive)
factors.summary: Boeing CEO signaled 200-jet China deal as 'initial tranche' with more to come. Modest positive for BA weight in Dow, but geopolitical overhang and rising rates limit upside enthusiasm.

factors.name: Credit Spreads & VIX Elevation
factors.summary: Geopolitical risk premium evident in FX volatility and commodity moves, but VIX not reported to spike dramatically yet. Suggests market is in early-stage repricing; further intraday escalation could trigger broader unwind.

key_levels.resistance: 50700–50800 (prior two-day range); break above requires risk-on reversal

screening.narrative: The Dow opened Thursday in a fragile near-flat state despite overnight US strikes on Iran, Iranian retaliation threats, and sharp commodity/FX repricing globally. The index is caught between persistent Fed hawkishness (Kashkari, Goolsbee on inflation stickiness) and geopolitical shock, with PCE data due at 12:30 GMT as the near-term pivot. Oil rebounded on Hormuz disruption fears, strengthening the dollar and pressuring multinational earnings translation; Asia weakness spilled over but Dow's flat action suggests investors are bid on dips, awaiting clarity on either an Iran deal or inflation data confirmation. This is PHASE_2 choppy positioning into a major data release.
catalysts_ahead: US Core PCE (April) — 12:30 GMT Thursday, May 28 (HIGH PRIORITY, expected to tick up per FXStreet)
Iran-US Ceasefire/Deal Negotiations — ongoing; any breakthrough or collapse statement will reset risk appetite
ECB low-tier releases (French PPI, Spanish Retail Sales) — expected muted reaction per news
Boeing earnings/guidance cycle — watch for China tariff and defense demand commentary
FOMC meeting (early June presumed) — forward guidance on rate path given inflation surprise
screening.risk_warning: If PCE comes in HOT (core >0.4% m/m or above expectations), Dow likely breaks down hard below 50400 on fresh hawkish repricing. Conversely, if Iran deal is announced or geopolitical tension subsides sharply, risk-on reversal could push Dow to 51000+. Avoid chasing current flat action; wait for PCE print or deal clarity for conviction entry.
screening.entry_guidance: Do not chase this flat open. Wait for PCE release (12:30 GMT) to establish conviction; if PCE is hot, short dips into 50600–50700 resistance. If deal announced or geopolitical risk fades, buy dips into 50400 support. Current PHASE_2 chop is a waiting room, not a tradeable setup.
